channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Daniel Dubois Reunites with Don Charles Ahead of WBO Title Fight Against Fabio Wardley
item.description: Daniel Dubois has reunited with trainer Don Charles following a brief split after his second defeat to Oleksandr Usyk in July at Wembley Stadium. The split had come as a surprise, given that Charles h...

item.title: Pope Leo XIV Announces First Pastoral Visit to Four African Countries
item.description: Pope Leo XIV will embark on a 10-day pastoral visit to Africa starting on 13 April, visiting Algeria, Cameroon, Angola, and Equatorial Guinea. This marks his first pastoral journey to the continent as...

item.title: Scientific review finds collagen supplements improve skin elasticity but do not prevent wrinkles
item.description: A new scientific review suggests that taking daily collagen supplements can improve skin elasticity and hydration, but they do not prevent wrinkles. Researchers analysed evidence from 113 clinical tri...

item.title: Evaluating the Benefits and Odds of Premium Bonds
item.description: The article was intended to explore whether investing in Premium Bonds is a wise choice, analysing the probability of winning and the potential returns for bondholders. Premium Bonds are a type of gov...

item.title: Global Trade Shifts: China Emerges as Leading Partner by 2024
item.description: A recent analysis of global trade patterns highlights significant changes in economic influence between the United States, the European Union, and China from 2000 to 2024. Maps created by Ehsan Soltan...

item.title: Singer D4vd Identified as Suspect in the Death of a 14-Year-Old Girl, Investigation Ongoing
item.description: Court documents recently unsealed in Texas have identified David Burke, professionally known as D4vd, as a suspect in the death of 14-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez. The filings indicate that Burke ...

item.title: Energy Security and the Druzhba Pipeline Dispute Behind Hungary and Slovakia’s EU Veto
item.description: The article analyzes the reasons behind Hungary and Slovakia’s veto of new European Union sanctions and a €90 billion credit package for Ukraine. While major German media outlets portray Hungarian Pri...

item.title: International Campaign Targets UN Rapporteur Francesca Albanese Over Gaza Report
item.description: The article discusses what it describes as an escalating international campaign against Francesca Albanese, the United Nations Special Rapporteur on the situation of human rights in the Palestinian te...

item.title: Trump Claims the U.S. Received Over 80 Million Barrels of Oil from Venezuela During State of the Union Address
item.description: According to the article published by mpr21 on February 25, 2026, former U.S. President Donald Trump stated during his State of the Union address that the United States had received more than 80 milli...

item.title: Venezuelan IT Worker Detained by Border Patrol Despite Employment
item.description: Angel Camacho, a Venezuelan asylum seeker and IT project manager with a master’s degree in telecommunications, was detained by U.S. Border Patrol after arriving to install a new intercom system at a F...

item.title: U.S. experiences net negative migration as Americans move abroad for safety and cost of living
item.description: For the first time in roughly 90 years, the United States has experienced a net negative migration, with more people leaving the country than moving in. According to the Brookings Institution, about 1...

item.title: Seth Meyers Predicts Trump Would Use Alien Life Announcement as Distraction from Epstein Files
item.description: Seth Meyers, in a July episode of 'Late Night with Seth Meyers', predicted that Donald Trump would announce the existence of extraterrestrial life to distract from the ongoing controversy surrounding ...

item.title: Rothschilds’ Connections in the Epstein Files Revealed
item.description: The Epstein Files, officially released by the U.S. Department of Justice in January 2026, contain 3.8 million pages with the name 'Rothschild' appearing nearly 12,000 times. Emails and contracts show ...

item.title: Russia warns of consequences if U.S. ends nuclear test moratorium
item.description: Three weeks after the expiration of the last major nuclear arms control treaty between the United States and Russia, tensions between the two powers have escalated. Speaking at the Geneva Disarmament ...

item.title: US Presses Iran to Dismantle Key Nuclear Facilities and Transfer Uranium in Geneva Talks
item.description: During the third round of indirect nuclear negotiations in Geneva, the United States reportedly demanded that Iran dismantle its primary nuclear facilities and transfer its entire stockpile of enriche...

item.title: Death of Blind Rohingya Refugee in Buffalo Raises Questions Over Border Patrol Release Procedures
item.description: A 56-year-old Rohingya refugee, Nurul Amin Shah Alam, was found dead on a street in Buffalo, New York, after being released by US Border Patrol approximately eight kilometres from his home in sub-zero...

item.title: I Exposed New Jersey's Jewish Invasion...
item.description: The video presents a highly controversial and likely unfounded claim of a 'Jewish invasion' of New Jersey, alleging secret land acquisitions and control by Jewish individuals and organizations. It use...

item.title: Hillary Clinton pauses Epstein deposition after photo leak during closed-door session

item.title: Robert DeNiro Expresses Emotional Criticism of Donald Trump on Podcast
